title: Psephellus pyrroblepharus
intro: There is limited information available for the plant Psephellus pyrroblepharus.
taxonomy: Psephellus pyrroblepharus belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant. The plant is a member of the genus Psephellus, which is closely related to the genus Crepis. The classification of Psephellus pyrroblepharus places it within the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. Psephellus pyrroblepharus is a type of thistle native to the Mediterranean region.
properties: Psephellus pyrroblepharus, a lesser-known species within the Asteraceae family, has been reported to possess various therapeutic properties. Studies suggest that it contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. These properties may contribute to its traditional use in folk medicine for treating various ailments, including wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. More studies are needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential and potential applications.

preparations: Psephellus pyrroblepharus, also known as the Golden Scabious, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula.
